game_name: Super Mario Bros.: The Lost Levels

design_philosophy.core_loop: Master pattern-based platforming through repeated attempts, learning enemy placement and level geometry, executing precise jumps and timing
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Split-second precision execution versus resource scarcity (limited lives); knowledge/pattern recognition versus unexpected traps (poison mushrooms, retrograde warps)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: For mastered Super Mario players: proving complete dominion over the platforming form by conquering expert-difficulty interpretations of familiar mechanics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Explicitly designed as a difficulty sequel rejecting accessibility in favor of demanding mastery. Introduces 'anti-progression' elements (poison mushrooms, warps that send players backward) and invisible block puzzles that punish exploration. No concession to casual players—labeled 'For Super Players.' This represents a deliberate design stance that challenge and exclusivity are virtues, not compromises.

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives (starts with finite supply); continue system available but resets progress to world start
difficulty.continues_system: Continue allows restart at beginning of current world after game over, but player must replay from world start rather than from checkpoint within world
difficulty.checkpointing: World-based checkpointing only; no mid-world checkpoints; each world's 4 levels must be completed in sequence without save points
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can choose Mario (faster, lower jump) or Luigi (slower, higher jump) to match skill level or level design. Warp zones available to skip worlds, but using warps blocks access to World 9. Hidden bonus worlds A-D unlock only after beating the game 8 times, creating meta-difficulty progression. No difficulty setting or assist modes in original release.

progression.upgrade_system: Power-ups only (Mushroom, Fire Flower, Invincibility Star); no persistent upgrades or unlockable abilities
progression.unlock_mechanism: World 9 unlocked by completing all 8 main worlds without using warp zones. Bonus worlds A-D unlocked after beating world 8-4 eight times. Progression deliberately gates content behind mastery requirement rather than narrative gates.
replayability_hooks: 52 total levels across 8 main + 5 bonus worlds
Character choice (Mario vs Luigi) creates two distinct mechanical playthroughs
Invisible block puzzles encourage replaying to discover all secrets
Warp zone discovery and world-skip routing creates speedrun optimization potential
High difficulty necessitates multiple attempts per level, enabling implicit speedrun practice
8-time completion requirement for bonus worlds creates explicit replayability gate

world_building_techniques: Toad's recurring 'princess is in another castle' dialogue maintains rescue narrative frame
Environmental hazard introduction (poison mushrooms, wind gusts, retrograde warps) suggests increasingly dangerous castle systems
World numbering (1-8 main, 9 hidden, A-D bonus) creates implicit geography of escalation

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as world-conclusion gates; minimal narrative significance, primarily mechanical obstacles. Bosses are known entities (Bowser, re-encountered multiple times) with increasing difficulty variants rather than unique designed encounters.
notable_bosses: Bowser (final world boss, encountered multiple times with increased difficulty across worlds)

faction_indicators: "For Super Players" packaging label explicitly marks this as content for the mastered/hardcore audience
Completion markers (8 world clears required for bonus access) create status indicators
Choice of Mario vs Luigi at start screen signals player skill assessment
danger_signals: Poison mushroom introduction signals hostile level design (anti-player mechanics)
Retrograde warps (level warps that return player to earlier worlds) punish exploration assumptions
Invisible block requirements indicate knowledge-gate mechanics unfamiliar to casual players
World 9 hidden behind no-warp-zone requirement signals meta-knowledge requirement
environmental_storytelling: Castle worlds escalate in visual/mechanical complexity
Poison mushroom visual (distinct from growth mushroom) creates environmental literacy requirement
Increasing enemy density and trap placement across worlds visualizes escalating danger

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Super Mario Bros. 2 (Famicom Disk System)
wikipedia - Super Mario Bros.: The Lost Levels Wikipedia article
